statusNote: DRAFT — awaiting Tom's sign-off. Flips to OPEN only after every item in `openChecklist` is satisfied (all PIN-BEFORE-OPEN fields filled with real values, split sealed, traced project cached). No eval may run while status != OPEN.
gatingDependency: Design doc above must be approved; L0 (real check_proof through the kernel) must be demonstrated on main — DONE 2026-06-27, see docs/06-Roadmap/Lean-L0-Trace-Deadlock.md §1c.

searchBudget.note: A pass@1 number is uninterpretable without the search budget. These are part of the registered protocol — fix them before any run; changing them later is a new experiment.

baselineColumn.name: one-shot whole-proof (honest floor)
baselineColumn.note: Run on the SAME split as a comparison column. A abstains heavily on hard problems (correct fail-closed behavior). The A-vs-B delta isolates the value of search and is the interesting number.

deferred.name: whole-proof + self-repair
deferred.note: A refinement on A, not an independent arm. Out of scope for the first run; would be registered as a separate experiment.

proposer.firstMilestone: Plumbing, not capability: 'B closes >=1 miniF2F-v2 problem end-to-end on a real LeanDojo session.' LeanProofSession.open() has never been exercised against a real lean-dojo install, so the first run is partly a LeanDojo-integration shakeout.

split.name: miniF2F-v2 (Lean 4)
split.sourceCorrection: CORRECTED 2026-06-27: the design doc's `facebookresearch/minif2f` is LEAN 3 (leanpkg.toml / `leanproject get-mathlib-cache`), NOT usable with our Lean 4 / lean-dojo 4.x stack. The Lean 4 'miniF2F-v2' is the miniF2F-Lean Revisited artifact (arXiv 2511.03108, Ospanov & Farnia); Lean 4 ports include yangky11/miniF2F-lean4 (lean-dojo author) and rahul3613/miniF2F-lean4. The exact v2-corrected Lean 4 release must be confirmed (the Revisited authors' own repo carries the v2 corrections; a generic port may be v1-era).
split.source: PIN-BEFORE-OPEN (Lean 4 miniF2F-v2 repo — confirm the Revisited-paper artifact vs a port)
split.commitShaNote: Pin the exact Lean 4 miniF2F-v2 commit at seal time and record it here. The number is meaningless without it. v1 numbers are NOT comparable to v2.

candidatePorts.yangky11/miniF2F-lean4: lean-toolchain leanprover/lean4:v4.24.0; mathlib4 rev f897ebcf72cd16f89ab4577d0c826cd14afaafc7 (NOTE: does NOT match lean-dojo's remote-cached mathlib4 — see tracedProject.cacheMatchRisk)
candidatePorts.rahul3613/miniF2F-lean4: ported from the original; pins UNVERIFIED — confirm whether it carries the v2 corrections

split.warmupAblationSplit: valid (244) — warmup/ablation ONLY, never cited as evidence (mirrors training-oracle vs evidence-oracle rule)

secondaryDomain.name: ProofNet
secondaryDomain.include: only if a usable Lean 4 port exists at run time; probes transfer beyond competition math
secondaryDomain.status: OPTIONAL — not required for the first run

split.license: Lean statements Apache-2.0 (Metamath portions MIT); eval use permitted with attribution + pinned revision in every artifact that cites a number.
split.honestResidual: miniF2F is public; the proposer model may have seen proofs in pretraining (the Revisited paper's whole point). We do NOT claim contamination-free; we claim the controls are documented and the residual risk is named. The clean-external path remains the (empty) agi-proof/third-party-heldout/ pack.

tracedProject.role: LOAD-BEARING: lean-dojo 4.x verification is traced-repo-keyed — check_proof resolves Theorem(LeanGitRepo(url, commit), file, name) against a TRACED Lean project, not a stateless snippet. The harness must point at a built/traced miniF2F Lean project (or cached trace), not pass a repo_url string.
tracedProject.leanToolchain: PIN-BEFORE-OPEN (the lean-toolchain the miniF2F Lean project pins)

ciCachePlan.mechanism: lean-dojo remote cache + actions/cache of ~/.cache/lean_dojo, keyed on leanProjectCommit — the SAME mechanism proven at L0 (Lean-L0-Trace-Deadlock.md §1c: 2.5GB mathlib4 cache, check_proof in ~3 min).
ciCachePlan.note: A CI cache of the traced project is a Phase-1 PREREQUISITE, not an afterthought — local cold tracing of mathlib is ~1 hr / 32 GB RAM (lean-dojo docs) and infeasible on a 2-core runner.

cacheMatchRisk.finding: INVESTIGATED 2026-06-27: the L0 ~3-min result depended on targeting lean-dojo's OWN cached mathlib4 commit (29dcec074... @ Lean v4.20.0). The miniF2F-v2 Lean 4 ports pin a DIFFERENT mathlib4 — yangky11/miniF2F-lean4 uses mathlib4 f897ebcf72cd16... @ Lean v4.24.0. lean-dojo traces a repo's full dependency closure, so tracing miniF2F re-traces its mathlib4 dep; cache reuse only happens if THAT mathlib4 commit is also in lean-dojo's remote cache.
cacheMatchRisk.implication: The L0 cache free-lunch does NOT automatically transfer to miniF2F-v2. Whether lean-dojo publishes a remote cache for the port's mathlib4 commit is UNVERIFIED (could not check — external git is blocked here, see openChecklist).
options: (a) Choose/produce a miniF2F-v2 Lean 4 build whose mathlib4 dep matches a lean-dojo-CACHED commit (ideal: matches 29dcec0/v4.20.0) → cheap CI trace like L0.
(b) Trace the port's mathlib4 once on a 32GB machine, persist ~/.cache/lean_dojo via actions/cache, then per-run tracing only touches the small miniF2F files.
(c) Confirm lean-dojo already caches the port's mathlib4 commit (download-only, like L0).

recipe.method: No training in this experiment — inference-time verifier-gated proof search (proposer B) vs one-shot baseline (A). (Distinct from the math/code curriculum, which is QLoRA SFT.)
recipe.knowledgeCutoffRule: Must be recorded and dated relative to the miniF2F-v2 revision used. We do not assert contamination-freedom; we assert the cutoff is documented and the residual risk named.
recipe.holdoutSeal: agi-proof/formal-proofs-curriculum/heldout-seal.manifest.json (created by the seal tool; not yet generated)

leakageControls.1_knowledgeCutoff: Proposer model cutoff recorded in `recipe.knowledgeCutoff`, dated before the miniF2F-v2 revision used. Residual risk named, not denied.
leakageControls.2_sealedSplit: miniF2F-v2 test (244) sealed to a SHA-256 hash manifest before any proposer runs; proposer data paths guarded by tools/heldout_seal_guard.py (extended) so it cannot read sealed items.
leakageControls.3_noExactLibraryLemma: A proof that closes by citing the target theorem's own Mathlib/library declaration name (e.g. `exact <goal_decl>`) is plagiarism of the answer, not evidence. The reward function rejects proofs whose closing step resolves to the goal statement's own declaration. This is the formal-proofs 'don't let the gate be the judge'.

trainingOracle.note: Mathlib lemmas may be used ONLY as a tactic-library / warmup source, NEVER as the evidence split (they are already proved by `exact <name>` and are public — worst-case contamination).

evidenceOracle.headlineMetric: pass@1 on miniF2F-v2 test (244), reported as a proportion with a 95% bootstrap CI, WITH proposer (B) and search budget named.
coReportedMetrics: abstention rate (first-class, never hidden — abstention is a designed output)
evidenceOracle.citableWhen: >=3 seeds, 95% bootstrap CI excludes the base/null, contamination CLEAN, lint_claims OK, failure-ledger entry present

thresholds.notes: No pre-committed target rate. The registered success is that the result clears the gate (>=3 seeds, CI methodology, controls) and is reported honestly with proposer+budget. A LOW pass@1 with HIGH abstention is a valid, expected, publishable outcome — the verifier doing its job — not a failure.

thresholds.metric: A-vs-B delta on miniF2F-v2 test pass@1
thresholds.notes: Isolates the value of search. Reported with CI; no pre-committed minimum — a null delta is informative.
Hard floor: any proof accepted that the Lean kernel does not actually close, or any `exact <goal_decl>` plagiarism accepted, is a protocol-breaking fabrication → abort + withdraw.

success.headline: pass@1 (A and B) + abstention on miniF2F-v2 test reported on >=3 seeds with a 95% CI and the proposer+budget named; contamination CLEAN; holdout seal OK; lint_claims OK; failure-ledger entry present.
success.explicitNonClaim: Clearing this gate is evidence of verifier-gated formal-proof capability on one split with one proposer at one budget. It does NOT move the Level ladder on its own and is NOT AGI evidence by itself.

failure: Any fabricated accept (kernel did not close the proof, or `exact <goal_decl>` plagiarism) → abort + withdraw headline (protocol violation).
Seal or contamination break → abort run / withdraw headline.
pass@1 statistically indistinguishable from baseline (CI overlaps) → record honest negative in the failure ledger; this is a VALID pre-registered outcome, not suppressed.
Proposer reads sealed held-out manifest paths → protocol violation.
A number reported without proposer + search budget named → uninterpretable, not citable.
falsification: miniF2F-v2 test pass@1 delta vs baseline <= 0 with CI including 0 across >=3 seeds (verifier-gated search shows no benefit at this budget).
heldout_seal_guard detects the proposer reading sealed paths.
A proof that the Lean kernel rejects is recorded as accepted (verifier integrity failure).
explicitNonClaims: NOT a Millennium-problems eval. open-problems.jsonl is an abstention demonstration only; no Riemann/Navier-Stokes/etc. framing appears in any committed result artifact.
NOT a wisdom / frontier-lab-comparison claim. No 'no frontier lab produces this', no 'wisdom-defining', no Level-ladder claim.
openChecklist: DONE (2026-06-27): Build tools/seal_formal_proofs_heldout.py + extend tools/heldout_seal_guard.py to cover the formal-proofs proposer paths. Validated by tests/test_formal_proofs_seal.py (fixture-level).

BLOCKED HERE (external git denied by org proxy, 403): Stage the real miniF2F-v2 test statements under private/formal-proofs-heldout/ (split + source.json), run tools/seal_formal_proofs_heldout.py, commit the hash manifest; this fills split.{source,commitSha} and --check must report CLEAN. Must be done in an environment WITH external git access (Tom's machine or a network-enabled CI job).
Confirm the Lean 4 miniF2F-v2 source: the Revisited-paper artifact (arXiv 2511.03108) vs a port (yangky11/ or rahul3613/miniF2F-lean4). NOT facebookresearch/minif2f (Lean 3).

Resolve tracedProject.cacheMatchRisk: pick option (a)/(b)/(c) — i.e. ensure the port's mathlib4 dep is lean-dojo-cacheable, else budget a one-off 32GB mathlib trace + actions/cache. Fill tracedProject.{leanProjectUrl, leanProjectCommit, leanToolchain}.
Fill proposer.primary.searchBudget.* (beam, depth, tactic-call cap, per-problem timeout).
Tom approves → flip status DRAFT → OPEN. Only then may any eval run.
